content_markdown: Which of the following changes on its own will increase the rate of evaporation of a liquid at constant temperature?

options.label: A
options.text: An increase in the surface area of the liquid

options.label: B
options.text: An increase in the total pressure acting on the liquid

options.label: C
options.text: A decrease in the surface area of the liquid

options.label: D
options.text: A decrease in the volume of the liquid

vibe_explanation: Evaporation occurs at the surface of a liquid. By increasing the surface area, more molecules are exposed to the surface at any given time, increasing the probability that high-energy molecules will escape the liquid phase into the gas phase. Increasing pressure or decreasing surface area would typically decrease the rate of evaporation, while volume itself does not directly affect the rate of evaporation per unit area.
